title: malus baccata subsp. fusca
intro: Malus baccata subsp. fusca, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Malus baccata subsp. fusca include the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and flu. Malus baccata subsp. fusca can be prepared in various forms, including tea, infusion, and tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
